---
title: "ECLIPS Internal Workshop 2"
author: "Frank DiTraglia & Ludovica Gazze"
institute: "Preliminary Results"
format: 
  beamer:
    aspectratio: 169
    theme: default
    header-includes: |
      \setbeamertemplate{footline}{\hfill\insertframenumber\,/\,\inserttotalframenumber\hspace{2em}\vspace{1ex}} 
      \usepackage{tikz}
      \usetikzlibrary{positioning}
      \usetikzlibrary{arrows}
      \usetikzlibrary{shapes}
      \usetikzlibrary{arrows.meta}
      \usetikzlibrary{fit}
      \usepackage{booktabs}
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
library(tidyverse)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE,
                      dev = "png", dpi = 300, fig.align = "center")

raw <- read_csv("~/ECLIPS-PAP/data/processed/combined-pseudonymized.csv",
                show_col_types = FALSE)

# Usable respondents only, with ordinal demographics coded as ordered factors
# (short display labels for the long survey categories).
surv <- raw |>
  filter(usable) |>
  mutate(
    prior_lead_knowledge = factor(prior_lead_knowledge,
      levels = c("Yes", "No", "Not sure")),
    worried_exposure = factor(worried_exposure,
      levels = c("Not worried", "A little worried", "Quite worried",
                 "Very worried", "Not sure", "Rather not say")),
    income = factor(income,
      levels = c("Under £15,000", "£15,000 - £24,999", "£25,000 - £34,999",
                 "£35,000 - £49,999", "£50,000 - £74,999", "£75,000 - £99,999",
                 "£100,000 - £149,999", "£150,000 and above",
                 "Not sure", "Rather not say")),
    highest_education = factor(highest_education,
      levels = c("No formal qualifications",
                 "Education up to age 16 (GCSE or equivalent)",
                 "Education up to age 18 (A-levels, high school diploma, baccalaureate, or equivalent)",
                 "Post-school qualification (non-degree) - e.g., diplomas, certificates, apprenticeships or other vocational training",
                 "Undergraduate degree (Bachelor's or equivalent)",
                 "Postgraduate degree (Master's, PhD or equivalent)",
                 "Rather not say"),
      labels = c("No formal quals", "Up to age 16 (GCSE)", "Up to age 18 (A-level)",
                 "Post-school (non-degree)", "Undergraduate", "Postgraduate",
                 "Rather not say")),
    home = factor(home,
      levels = c("Owned by you (either with a mortgage or outright)",
                 "Rented from the council", "Rented from a private landlord",
                 "Living with family or friends", "Other:", "Not sure",
                 "Rather not say"),
      labels = c("Owned", "Rented (council)", "Rented (private)",
                 "With family/friends", "Other", "Not sure", "Rather not say")),
    ethnicity = factor(ethnicity,
      levels = c("White (British [English, Welsh, Scottish, Northern Irish], Irish, Gypsy or Irish Traveller, Roma, any other White background)",
                 "Asian or Asian British (Indian, Pakistani, Bangladeshi, Chinese, any other Asian background)",
                 "Black, Black British, Caribbean or African (Caribbean, African, any other Black background)",
                 "Mixed or multiple backgrounds (White and Black Caribbean, White and Black African, White and Asian, any other mixed background)",
                 "Other (Arab, any other ethnicity)",
                 "Rather not say"),
      labels = c("White", "Asian / Asian British", "Black / Black British",
                 "Mixed", "Other", "Rather not say"))
  )

# Cell-level usable-response rates (mirrors 2026-04-16 combined analysis §2.3/§3):
# denominator = letters mailed per cell; rate per 1,000 with Wilson score CI.
cell_levels <- c("NonOld_C", "NonOld_G", "Old_C", "Old_G", "Old_P")
ci_level   <- 0.95                                 # CI level used everywhere below
ci_pct     <- paste0(round(100 * ci_level), "%")   # e.g. "95%"
ci_caption <- paste0("Bars: ", ci_pct, " Wilson score intervals")

# Point = observed rate (x/n); bounds = Wilson score interval (= prop.test, no cc).
score_ci <- function(x, n, cl = 0.95) {
  z <- qnorm(1 - (1 - cl) / 2)
  p <- x / n
  ctr  <- (p + z^2 / (2 * n)) / (1 + z^2 / n)
  half <- (z * sqrt(p * (1 - p) / n + z^2 / (4 * n^2))) / (1 + z^2 / n)
  c(est = 1000 * p, lo = 1000 * (ctr - half), hi = 1000 * (ctr + half))
}

mailed_per_cell <- read_csv("~/ECLIPS-PAP/data/processed/addresses-and-treatments.csv",
                            show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  mutate(cell = paste(house_type, treatment, sep = "_")) |>
  count(cell, name = "n_mailed")

cell_table <- mailed_per_cell |>
  left_join(
    raw |>
      mutate(cell = paste(house_type_parsed, treatment_parsed, sep = "_")) |>
      group_by(cell) |>
      summarise(n_responded = n(), n_usable = sum(usable), .groups = "drop"),
    by = "cell"
  ) |>
  rowwise() |>
  mutate(ci = list(score_ci(n_usable, n_mailed, cl = ci_level))) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(per_1000 = map_dbl(ci, "est"),
         lo = map_dbl(ci, "lo"),
         hi = map_dbl(ci, "hi"),
         cell = factor(cell, levels = cell_levels)) |>
  arrange(cell)

# --- IMD (deprivation) gradient: usable rate by IMD quintile ---
# imd_score higher = more deprived; quintiles = equal fifths of the 488 Leeds LSOAs.
lsoa_imd <- read_csv("~/ECLIPS-PAP/data/processed/lsoa-reference-table.csv",
                     show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  select(lsoa = lsoa21cd, imd_score)

# age_grp splits the design's Non-Old into New (known >= 1967) vs Unknown (no EPC).
addr_imd <- read_csv("~/ECLIPS-PAP/data/processed/addresses-and-treatments.csv",
                     show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  mutate(age_grp = case_when(house_type == "Old"                   ~ "Old",
                             !is.na(age_end) & age_end > 1966       ~ "New",
                             TRUE                                   ~ "Unknown")) |>
  select(lsoa, treatment, age_grp) |>
  left_join(lsoa_imd, by = "lsoa")

imd_breaks <- quantile(lsoa_imd$imd_score, probs = seq(0, 1, 0.2), na.rm = TRUE)
bin_imd <- function(x) cut(x, imd_breaks, include.lowest = TRUE,
                           labels = c("Q1\nleast", "Q2", "Q3", "Q4", "Q5\nmost"))

imd_cells <- addr_imd |>
  mutate(q = bin_imd(imd_score)) |>
  filter(!is.na(q)) |>
  count(q, age_grp, treatment, name = "mailed") |>
  left_join(
    raw |>
      mutate(age_grp = recode(age_status, "Known Non-Old" = "New",
                              "Unknown Age" = "Unknown")) |>
      select(lsoa, treatment = treatment_parsed, age_grp, usable) |>
      left_join(lsoa_imd, by = "lsoa") |>
      mutate(q = bin_imd(imd_score)) |>
      filter(!is.na(q)) |>
      group_by(q, age_grp, treatment) |>
      summarise(usable = sum(usable), clicks = n(), .groups = "drop"),
    by = c("q", "age_grp", "treatment")
  ) |>
  mutate(across(c(usable, clicks), \(x) replace_na(x, 0L)))

# rate per 1,000 + Wilson CI for an IMD-quintile grouping; outcome = usable or clicks.
imd_summary <- function(df, extra = character(0), outcome = "usable") {
  df |>
    group_by(across(all_of(c("q", extra)))) |>
    summarise(mailed = sum(mailed), num = sum(.data[[outcome]]), .groups = "drop") |>
    rowwise() |>
    mutate(ci = list(score_ci(num, mailed, cl = ci_level))) |>
    ungroup() |>
    mutate(rate = map_dbl(ci, "est"), lo = map_dbl(ci, "lo"), hi = map_dbl(ci, "hi"))
}

# --- Map data: Leeds LSOA (2021) boundaries + per-LSOA quantities ---
# Boundaries from ONS (BGC, OGL v3); see ../data/leeds-lsoa-2021-bgc-PROVENANCE.md.
library(sf)
leeds_geo <- st_read("../data/leeds-lsoa-2021-bgc.geojson", quiet = TRUE)

# Estimated eligible share (E_l, the Hamilton sampling weight) per LSOA.
lsoa_elig <- read_csv("~/ECLIPS-PAP/data/processed/lsoa-reference-table.csv",
                      show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  select(lsoa = lsoa21cd, E_l)

# Letters mailed per LSOA across both rounds (addr_imd = one row per mailed address).
letters_by_lsoa <- addr_imd |> count(lsoa, name = "n_letters")

# Empirical-Bayes (Beta-Binomial) shrinkage of per-LSOA usable-response rates.
# y usable of n letters per LSOA; p ~ Beta(a, b) with (a, b) by marginal MLE.
eb_lsoa <- letters_by_lsoa |>
  rename(n = n_letters) |>
  left_join(raw |> filter(usable) |> count(lsoa, name = "y"), by = "lsoa") |>
  mutate(y = replace_na(y, 0L))
eb_nll <- function(p) {
  a <- exp(p[1]); b <- exp(p[2])
  -sum(lchoose(eb_lsoa$n, eb_lsoa$y) +
       lbeta(eb_lsoa$y + a, eb_lsoa$n - eb_lsoa$y + b) - lbeta(a, b))
}
eb_fit <- optim(c(log(0.3), log(50)), eb_nll)
eb_a <- exp(eb_fit$par[1]); eb_b <- exp(eb_fit$par[2])
eb_lsoa <- eb_lsoa |>
  mutate(raw = 1000 * y / n, post = 1000 * (y + eb_a) / (n + eb_a + eb_b))

# Horizontal count bar chart for a (factor) survey variable; first level on top.
# True NAs (item-nonresponse) are folded into "Rather not say".
plot_bar <- function(var, base = 12) {
  f <- surv[[var]]
  if (!"Rather not say" %in% levels(f)) levels(f) <- c(levels(f), "Rather not say")
  f[is.na(f)] <- "Rather not say"
  tibble(val = f) |>
    count(val, .drop = FALSE) |>
    filter(n > 0) |>
    ggplot(aes(x = n, y = fct_rev(val))) +
    geom_col(fill = "#3B6E8F") +
    geom_text(aes(label = n), hjust = -0.25, size = 3) +
    scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15))) +
    labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = base) +
    theme(panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
          panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
}
```

# Overview

## Recruitment \& Treatment Allocation 

\vspace{1em}
\centering
\begin{tikzpicture}[
    every node/.style={align=center},
    block/.style={draw, rounded corners, fill=blue!8, minimum width=3.2cm,
                  minimum height=2cm, font=\small},
    >={Stealth[length=3mm]}]

  \node (sampling) [block] {\textbf{1. Sampling}\\\textbf{Design}};
  \node (intake) [block, right=1.4cm of sampling] {\textbf{2. Recruitment}\\\textbf{\& Intake Survey}};
  \node (kits) [block, right=1.4cm of intake] {\textbf{3. Kit Allocation,}\\\textbf{\& Exposure Survey}};

  \draw[->, thick] (sampling) -- (intake);
  \draw[->, thick] (intake) -- (kits);
\end{tikzpicture}


## Who responds and why?

- Various kinds of responses: intake survey, exposure survey, return kit, complete experience survey, etc. 
- Design of recruiting materials: randomized controlled trial
- Design of the kits: randomized controlled trial
- Demographic characteristics: observational 



## Description of outcomes

### Started
Opened the baseline survey within 45 days of the letter 

### Usable Response
Eligible child (aged 1--6), valid consent, and GP details provided

### Exposure Survey
Completed survey (preliminary; deadline not yet specified)

### Returned Kit
Blood-spot card came back and was lab-assessed (preliminary; deadline not yet specified)

### Valid Sample
One or more usable blood spot on the returned card

# 1 - Sampling Design

## Sampling Design: Data Sources

\hypertarget{main-datasources}{}

### Leeds Council Tax Data: 
<!--`leeds_council_tax_data.csv`-->
- Source of residential addresses in Leeds
- 376,205 rows: one per chargeable dwelling
- Columns we use: Address 1-4, Unique Property Reference Number (UPRN)


### ONS address data: 
<!--`ONS_UPRN_NOV_2024_YH.csv`-->
Merge Postcode, OA, LSOA and MSOA with Council Tax data (by UPRN)

### Energy Performance Certificates (EPC): 
<!--`EPC_certificates.csv`-->
Merge Housing age (use latest certificate) with Council Tax data (by UPRN)

### Census Data (2021) at LSOA level 
Merge LSOA-level demographics with Council Tax datai (by LSOA)

### Exclusion / De-duplication
\hyperlink{appx-exclusion}{\beamergotobutton{See appendix slide for details}}


## Big differences in HHs with kids across LSOA

```{r}
#| label: lsoa-eligible-share-hist
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 3.5
read_csv("~/ECLIPS-PAP/data/processed/lsoa-reference-table.csv",
         show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  mutate(elig_rate = households_0_9 / total_households) |>
  ggplot(aes(elig_rate)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 40, fill = "#3B6E8F", colour = "white", linewidth = 0.1) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 1)) +
  labs(x = "Eligible HH in LSOA / Total HH in LSOA", y = "Number of LSOAs") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
```

Estimated from 2021 Census (children aged 0--9), one observation per LSOA.


## Stratified Random Sampling to Improve Precision

\alert{How to allocate a total of $N$ letters across LSOAs / housing age?}

### Eligibility
- Letters sent to households without a child aged 1-6 are wasted
- Approximate \# eligible from 2021 Census: 0-4 \& 5-9 bands
- $E_\ell =$ (\# Eligible in LSOA $\ell$) / (Total \# Eligible)
- Send $N \times E_\ell$ letters to LSOA $\ell$ (Hamilton allocation)


### Housing Age
- $O_\ell=$ Share of Old houses in LSOA $\ell$
- Suppose $N_\ell$ letters will go to LSOA $\ell$ based on eligibility.
- Send $N_\ell \times O_\ell$ of the $N_\ell$ letters go to Old houses
- The rest go to NonOld houses (Hamilton allocation) 



  

## Where we sent letters

```{r}
#| label: letters-map-and-scatter
#| layout-ncol: 2
#| fig-width: 4.6
#| fig-height: 4.6
library(patchwork)

# (left) Where letters went: choropleth of letters mailed per LSOA (raw counts).
leeds_geo |>
  left_join(letters_by_lsoa, by = c("LSOA21CD" = "lsoa")) |>
  ggplot(aes(fill = n_letters)) +
  geom_sf(colour = "white", linewidth = 0.03) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "mako", direction = -1, name = "Letters",
                       na.value = "grey85") +
  coord_sf(datum = NA) +
  theme_void(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "right", legend.key.width = unit(0.3, "cm"))

# (right) Why: letters per LSOA vs estimated eligible share, with marginals.
d   <- letters_by_lsoa |> inner_join(lsoa_elig, by = "lsoa")
col <- "#3B6E8F"

main <- ggplot(d, aes(E_l, n_letters)) +
  geom_abline(slope = sum(d$n_letters), intercept = 0,
              linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey55") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.45, colour = col, size = 1.1) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 0.1)) +
  labs(x = "Eligible in LSOA / Total Eligible", y = "Letters mailed") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

top   <- ggplot(d, aes(E_l)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 40, fill = col, colour = "white", linewidth = 0.1) +
  theme_void()

right <- ggplot(d, aes(n_letters)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 40, fill = col, colour = "white", linewidth = 0.1) +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_void()

top + plot_spacer() + main + right +
  plot_layout(ncol = 2, nrow = 2, widths = c(4, 1), heights = c(1, 4))
```


## Letters sent vs. LSOA deprivation

```{r}
#| label: letters-vs-deprivation
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 3.8
letters_by_lsoa |>
  inner_join(lsoa_imd, by = "lsoa") |>
  mutate(share = n_letters / sum(n_letters)) |>
  ggplot(aes(imd_score, share)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.45, colour = "#3B6E8F", size = 1.1) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, colour = "#B5651D",
              fill = "#B5651D", alpha = 0.15, linewidth = 0.7) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 0.1)) +
  labs(x = "IMD score  (higher = more deprived)",
       y = "Share of all letters sent") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
```


## Randomized Controlled Trial \#1: Recruiting Letter


### Generalized Risk (G)
> The UK Health Security Agency says that lead can be found in things like: Paint on old walls, doors, windowsills, radiators, furniture, toys, and ornaments; Old plumbing, including pipes, tanks, and taps; Some spices brought from other countries; Some traditional medicines and beauty products (like surma or kohl eye makeup); Certain cooking pots (including grey pewter pots) from countries outside the UK and Europe. 

### Personalized Risk (P)
> We see from local records that your home was built before 1967, so it may have old paint or pipes containing lead. Finding out if your child has come into contact with lead could help protect their health. 

\vspace{1em}
\alert{Control letter contains neither; Personalized Risk letter contains both}


---

![](../img/personalized-risk-letter-2page.png){fig-align="center"}

## Randomization of Letter Types

Recall: Randomization: "personalized" treatment only for Old homes 

- Old stratum: 1/3 control, 1/3 generalized, 1/3 personalized
- NonOld stratum: 1/2 control, 1/2 generalized
  
## Adaptive Design

\alert{Budget for 150k letters and 2k baseline surveys (5 GBP gift card)}

\vspace{1em}

### Round 1  
Send 30k letters; wait 45 days; \# of responses $R_1$ determines letters sent in Round 2 

### Round 2 
- Plug-in response rate $\hat{p} = R_1 / 30{,}000$; send enough to reach 2,000:
  $$\text{send} = \min\!\Big(120\text{k},\ 10\text{k}\times\big\lceil (2000 -
  R_1)\,/\,(10{,}000\,\hat{p})\big\rceil\Big)$$
- Round **up** to next 10k; stop early if $R_1 \geq 2{,}000$ (target met) or $R_1 < 20$ (disaster)

### Realized Allocation

- Based on Round 1 Response Rate: sent 120k letters in Round 2 (150k total)


# 2 - Recruitment \& Intake Survey Results


## Phase 1: Outreach \& Intake Survey

\vspace{1em}

\centering
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.5, every node/.style={align=center}]
  \node (addr) [draw] {All Leeds Residential Addresses \\ $370{,}666$};
  \node (letters) [draw, below of=addr, node distance=2.2cm] {Send $150{,}000$ Letters};
  \node (started) [draw, below right of=letters, node distance=2.7cm] {$1{,}645$ \\ Started Survey};
  \node (noresp) [draw, dashed, below left of=letters, node distance=2.7cm] {No Response \\ $148{,}355$};
  \node (usable) [draw, below right of=started, node distance=2.7cm] {$890$ \\ Usable Responses};
  \node (drop) [draw, dashed, below left of=started, node distance=2.7cm] {Ineligible or \\ no consent \\ $755$};

  \draw[->] (addr) -- (letters) node[midway, right]{Stratified Random Sample ($\approx 40\%$)};
  \draw[->] (letters) -- (started) node[midway, right]{$1.1\%$};
  \draw[->, dashed] (letters) -- (noresp);
  \draw[->] (started) -- (usable) node[midway, right]{$54\%$};
  \draw[->, dashed] (started) -- (drop);
\end{tikzpicture}



## Usable response rates vary with housing age not treatment.

```{r}
#| label: cell-rate-forest
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 3.9
cell_table |>
  mutate(
    house  = if_else(str_starts(as.character(cell), "Old"), "Old", "Non-Old"),
    letter = recode(str_sub(as.character(cell), start = -1L),
                    C = "Control", G = "Generalized", P = "Personalized"),
    label  = factor(paste0(house, ": ", letter),
                    levels = rev(c("Non-Old: Control", "Non-Old: Generalized",
                                   "Old: Control", "Old: Generalized",
                                   "Old: Personalized")))
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = per_1000, y = label, colour = house)) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = lo, xmax = hi), height = 0.22, linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("Old" = "#B5651D", "Non-Old" = "#3B6E8F")) +
  labs(x = "Usable responses per 1,000 letters mailed", y = NULL, colour = NULL,
       caption = ci_caption) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        plot.caption = element_text(size = 9, colour = "grey40"))
```

## Highest response from Old (pre 1967); lowest from Unknown (no EPC)

```{r}
#| label: age-status-forest
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 3.6
age_levels <- c("Old", "New", "Unknown")

age_tab <- read_csv("~/ECLIPS-PAP/data/processed/addresses-and-treatments.csv",
                    show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  mutate(age_grp = case_when(
    house_type == "Old"               ~ "Old",
    !is.na(age_end) & age_end > 1966  ~ "New",
    TRUE                              ~ "Unknown")) |>
  count(age_grp, treatment, name = "mailed") |>
  left_join(
    raw |>
      filter(usable) |>
      mutate(age_grp = recode(age_status,
                              "Known Non-Old" = "New", "Unknown Age" = "Unknown")) |>
      count(age_grp, treatment = treatment_parsed, name = "usable"),
    by = c("age_grp", "treatment")
  ) |>
  rowwise() |>
  mutate(ci = list(score_ci(usable, mailed, cl = ci_level))) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(per_1000 = map_dbl(ci, "est"),
         lo = map_dbl(ci, "lo"),
         hi = map_dbl(ci, "hi"),
         age_grp = factor(age_grp, levels = rev(age_levels)),
         treatment = recode(treatment, C = "Control", G = "Generalized",
                            P = "Personalized"),
         treatment = factor(treatment,
                            levels = c("Control", "Generalized", "Personalized")))

ggplot(age_tab, aes(x = per_1000, y = age_grp, colour = treatment)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 2.5, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey55") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = lo, xmax = hi),
                 position = position_dodge(width = 0.6), height = 0.2, linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_point(position = position_dodge(width = 0.6), size = 2.8) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("Control" = "#666666",
                                 "Generalized" = "#3B6E8F",
                                 "Personalized" = "#B5651D")) +
  labs(x = "Usable responses per 1,000 letters mailed", y = NULL, colour = NULL,
       caption = paste0("dashed line = design Old/Non-Old cut; ", ci_caption)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        plot.caption = element_text(size = 9, colour = "grey40"))
```



## Treatment affects *starting* the survey. \hfill \small \alert{(Preliminary)}


```{r}
#| label: click-rate-forest
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 3.4
cell_table |>
  rowwise() |>
  mutate(ci = list(score_ci(n_responded, n_mailed, cl = ci_level))) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(
    click  = map_dbl(ci, "est"),
    clo    = map_dbl(ci, "lo"),
    chi    = map_dbl(ci, "hi"),
    house  = if_else(str_starts(as.character(cell), "Old"), "Old", "Non-Old"),
    letter = recode(str_sub(as.character(cell), start = -1L),
                    C = "Control", G = "Generalized", P = "Personalized"),
    label  = factor(paste0(house, ": ", letter),
                    levels = rev(c("Non-Old: Control", "Non-Old: Generalized",
                                   "Old: Control", "Old: Generalized",
                                   "Old: Personalized")))
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = click, y = label, colour = house)) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = clo, xmax = chi), height = 0.22, linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("Old" = "#B5651D", "Non-Old" = "#3B6E8F")) +
  labs(x = "Any response (clicked / started) per 1,000 letters mailed",
       y = NULL, colour = NULL, caption = ci_caption) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        plot.caption = element_text(size = 9, colour = "grey40"))
```


## Treatment effects *starting* the survey. \hfill \small \alert{(Preliminary)}

```{r}
#| label: click-diff-forest
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 2.8
library(PropCIs)

diff_ci <- function(x1, n1, x2, n2, cl = ci_level) {
  d <- diffscoreci(x1, n1, x2, n2, conf.level = cl)
  c(est = 1000 * (x1 / n1 - x2 / n2),
    lo  = 1000 * d$conf.int[1], hi = 1000 * d$conf.int[2])
}

v <- cell_table |> transmute(cell = as.character(cell), n_responded, n_mailed)
get <- function(c, col) v[[col]][v$cell == c]

tibble(
  contrast = c("Non-Old:  G − C", "Old:  (G + P) − C"),
  x1 = c(get("NonOld_G", "n_responded"),
         get("Old_G", "n_responded") + get("Old_P", "n_responded")),
  n1 = c(get("NonOld_G", "n_mailed"),
         get("Old_G", "n_mailed") + get("Old_P", "n_mailed")),
  x2 = c(get("NonOld_C", "n_responded"), get("Old_C", "n_responded")),
  n2 = c(get("NonOld_C", "n_mailed"),    get("Old_C", "n_mailed"))
) |>
  rowwise() |>
  mutate(d = list(diff_ci(x1, n1, x2, n2))) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(est = map_dbl(d, "est"), lo = map_dbl(d, "lo"), hi = map_dbl(d, "hi"),
         contrast = factor(contrast, levels = rev(c("Non-Old:  G − C",
                                                    "Old:  (G + P) − C")))) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = est, y = contrast)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey55") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = lo, xmax = hi), height = 0.15, linewidth = 0.8,
                 colour = "#3B6E8F") +
  geom_point(size = 3.2, colour = "#3B6E8F") +
  labs(x = "Difference in clicks per 1,000 letters  (treatment − control)",
       y = NULL,
       caption = paste0(ci_pct, " Newcombe score intervals")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        plot.caption = element_text(size = 9, colour = "grey40"))
```



## Intake Survey: Awareness of Lead

\small Usable respondents ($N = 890$); counts shown on bars.

```{r}
#| label: lead-awareness
#| layout-ncol: 2
#| fig-width: 4.6
#| fig-height: 3.4
plot_bar("prior_lead_knowledge") + ggtitle("Heard of lead risk before?")
plot_bar("worried_exposure") + ggtitle("Worried about exposure?")
```

## Intake Survey: Socioeconomic Profile


```{r}
#| label: ses-profile
#| layout-ncol: 2
#| fig-width: 4.9
#| fig-height: 3.6
plot_bar("income") + ggtitle("Household income")
plot_bar("highest_education") + ggtitle("Highest education")
```

## Intake Survey: Housing \& Ethnicity

```{r}
#| label: housing-ethnicity
#| layout-ncol: 2
#| fig-width: 4.9
#| fig-height: 3.6
plot_bar("home") + ggtitle("Housing tenure")
plot_bar("ethnicity") + ggtitle("Ethnicity")
```


## Lower response rate in more deprived LSOAs

\hypertarget{main-deprivation}{}

```{r}
#| label: usable-imd-marginal
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 3.3
imd_summary(imd_cells) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = q, y = rate, group = 1)) +
  geom_line(colour = "#3B6E8F", linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_pointrange(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), colour = "#3B6E8F", size = 0.5) +
  labs(x = "IMD quintile  (left = least deprived)",
       y = "Usable per 1,000", caption = ci_caption) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.caption = element_text(size = 9, colour = "grey40"),
        plot.margin = margin(t = 10, r = 8, b = 4, l = 6))
```

\footnotesize Background:
\hyperlink{appx-imd}{\beamergotobutton{What is IMD?}}
\hyperlink{appx-imd-dist}{\beamergotobutton{England vs Leeds}}
\hyperlink{appx-imd-map}{\beamergotobutton{Leeds deprivation map}}
\hyperlink{appx-eb}{\beamergotobutton{Per-LSOA maps \& shrinkage}}

## ... but mainly driven by Old houses

\hypertarget{main-by-age}{}

```{r}
#| label: usable-imd-by-age
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 3.3
imd_summary(imd_cells, "age_grp", outcome = "usable") |>
  mutate(age_grp = factor(age_grp, levels = c("Old", "New", "Unknown"))) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = q, y = rate, colour = age_grp, group = age_grp)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.6, position = position_dodge(0.12)) +
  geom_pointrange(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), size = 0.45,
                  position = position_dodge(0.12)) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("Old" = "#B5651D", "New" = "#3B6E8F",
                                 "Unknown" = "#7A7A7A")) +
  labs(x = "IMD quintile  (left = least deprived)",
       y = "Usable per 1,000", colour = NULL, caption = ci_caption) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        plot.caption = element_text(size = 9, colour = "grey40"),
        plot.margin = margin(t = 10, r = 8, b = 4, l = 6))
```

\footnotesize \hyperlink{appx-imd-treat}{\beamergotobutton{Does the gradient differ by treatment?}}
\hyperlink{appx-click-age}{\beamergotobutton{Clicks version of this plot}}

## More Old housing in deprived LSOAs areas

```{r}
#| label: stock-composition
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 3.6
addr_imd |>
  mutate(q = bin_imd(imd_score)) |>
  filter(!is.na(q)) |>
  count(q, age_grp) |>
  mutate(age_grp = factor(age_grp, levels = c("Old", "New", "Unknown"))) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = q, y = n, fill = age_grp)) +
  geom_col(position = "fill", width = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Old" = "#B5651D", "New" = "#3B6E8F",
                               "Unknown" = "#7A7A7A")) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_percent()) +
  labs(x = "IMD quintile  (left = least deprived)",
       y = "Share of letters mailed", fill = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

## Responder demographics versus LSOA deprivation

```{r}
#| label: responder-traits-imd
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 3.9
ind_levels <- c("Low income (≤£25k)", "Renting", "Low education", "Non-White")

raw |>
  filter(usable) |>
  left_join(lsoa_imd, by = "lsoa") |>
  mutate(
    q = bin_imd(imd_score),
    `Low income (≤£25k)` = income %in% c("Under £15,000", "£15,000 - £24,999"),
    `Renting`            = home %in% c("Rented from the council",
                                       "Rented from a private landlord"),
    `Low education`      = highest_education %in%
      c("No formal qualifications", "Education up to age 16 (GCSE or equivalent)"),
    `Non-White`          = !is.na(ethnicity) & !str_starts(ethnicity, "White")
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(q)) |>
  select(q, all_of(ind_levels)) |>
  pivot_longer(-q, names_to = "indicator", values_to = "flag") |>
  group_by(indicator, q) |>
  summarise(x = sum(flag, na.rm = TRUE), n = sum(!is.na(flag)), .groups = "drop") |>
  rowwise() |>
  mutate(ci = list(score_ci(x, n, cl = ci_level))) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(pct = map_dbl(ci, "est") / 10,    # score_ci is per-1,000; /10 -> percent
         lo  = map_dbl(ci, "lo") / 10,
         hi  = map_dbl(ci, "hi") / 10,
         indicator = factor(indicator, levels = ind_levels)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = q, y = pct, group = 1)) +
  geom_line(colour = "#3B6E8F", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_pointrange(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), colour = "#3B6E8F", size = 0.3) +
  facet_wrap(~ indicator, nrow = 1) +
  labs(x = "IMD quintile  (left = least deprived)",
       y = "% of responders", caption = ci_caption) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(plot.caption = element_text(size = 9, colour = "grey40"))
```

# 3 - Kit Allocation and Exposure Survey

## Kit Allocation

\footnotesize

### Flag as "disadvantaged" if *any* of the following:
- Low income: $\leq$ £24,999
- Low education:  "No formal qualifications" or "Education up to age 16"
- Language: English non-native *and* reading/writing "Quite hard" / "Very hard"

### Re-balancing
- Budgeted for 500 kits and associated lab capacity
- Expect fewer responses from some groups, so recruit more participants than kits
- Earmark 50\% of kits for "disadvantaged"; choose randomly among them
- Allocate remaining kits randomly among not disadvantaged

### Randomize Kit Types
- 50-50 random assignment *stratified* by (Letter treatment) $\times$ (Disadvantaged)

### Across rounds (realized)
- Split 500 kits across rounds $\propto$ expected responses; selection probabilities round-specific
- All disadvantaged got a kit: 100 kits / 49 disadvantaged (R1); 400 kits / 224 (R2)

## Where we sent kits

```{r}
#| label: kits-map-and-deprivation
#| layout-ncol: 2
#| fig-width: 4.6
#| fig-height: 4.6
kits_by_lsoa <- read_csv("master_dataset_noaddr.csv", show_col_types = FALSE,
                         name_repair = "minimal") |>
  filter(!is.na(treatment_kit)) |>
  count(lsoa, name = "n_kits")

# (left) Where kits went: choropleth of kits sent per LSOA.
leeds_geo |>
  left_join(kits_by_lsoa, by = c("LSOA21CD" = "lsoa")) |>
  ggplot(aes(fill = n_kits)) +
  geom_sf(colour = "white", linewidth = 0.03) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "mako", direction = -1, name = "Kits",
                       na.value = "grey85") +
  coord_sf(datum = NA) +
  theme_void(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "right", legend.key.width = unit(0.3, "cm"))

# (right) Kits sent per LSOA vs deprivation (all 488 LSOAs; 0 if none).
lsoa_imd |>
  left_join(kits_by_lsoa, by = "lsoa") |>
  mutate(n_kits = replace_na(n_kits, 0L)) |>
  ggplot(aes(imd_score, n_kits)) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0, height = 0.18, alpha = 0.4, colour = "#3B6E8F",
              size = 1.1) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, colour = "#B5651D",
              fill = "#B5651D", alpha = 0.15, linewidth = 0.7) +
  labs(x = "IMD score  (higher = more deprived)", y = "Kits sent in LSOA") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
```

## Phase II: Collecting Blood Samples

\centering
\resizebox{\textwidth}{!}{%
\begin{tikzpicture}[every node/.style={draw, align=center, font=\small}, >=stealth]
  \node (usable) at (0,0)     {890 Usable Respondents};
  \node (kits)   at (0,-1.6)  {Send 500 Kits + Exposure Survey};
  \node (kitA)   at (-3.3,-3.4) {Kit A \\ 251};
  \node (kitB)   at (3.3,-3.4)  {Kit B \\ 249};
  \node (Aret)   at (-5,-5.7)  {Returned kit \\ 79 (31\%) \\ {\scriptsize 59 valid}};
  \node (Asur)   at (-1.7,-5.7) {Exposure survey \\ 82 (33\%)};
  \node (Bret)   at (1.7,-5.7)  {Returned kit \\ 75 (30\%) \\ {\scriptsize 58 valid}};
  \node (Bsur)   at (5,-5.7)   {Exposure survey \\ 74 (30\%)};

  \draw[->] (usable) -- (kits) node[midway, right, draw=none]{re-balanced $\approx 56\%$};
  \draw[->] (kits) -- (kitA);
  \draw[->] (kits) -- (kitB) node[midway, above right, draw=none]{randomize kit type};
  \draw[->] (kitA) -- (Aret);
  \draw[->] (kitA) -- (Asur);
  \draw[->] (kitB) -- (Bret);
  \draw[->] (kitB) -- (Bsur);
\end{tikzpicture}%
}

## No Effect of Kit Type \hfill \small \alert{(Preliminary)}

```{r}
#| label: kit-type-forest
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 3.6
# Among the 500 kit recipients (treatment_kit non-NA). valid_sample = number of
# valid blood spots (0/1/2), confirmed against the DBS L/R validation columns.
kit <- read_csv("master_dataset_noaddr.csv", show_col_types = FALSE,
                name_repair = "minimal") |>
  filter(!is.na(treatment_kit)) |>
  mutate(
    kit_type = factor(kit_type, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("Kit 0", "Kit 1")),
    `Completed exposure survey` = completed_exposure_survey == 1,
    `Returned a kit (any)`      = !is.na(DBSLvalidation) | !is.na(DBSRvalidation),
    `Returned >=1 valid spot`   = valid_sample >= 1)

outcomes <- c("Completed exposure survey", "Returned a kit (any)",
              "Returned >=1 valid spot")

kit |>
  pivot_longer(all_of(outcomes), names_to = "outcome", values_to = "hit") |>
  group_by(kit_type, outcome) |>
  summarise(x = sum(hit), n = n(), .groups = "drop") |>
  rowwise() |>
  mutate(ci = list(score_ci(x, n, cl = ci_level))) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(est = map_dbl(ci, "est") / 10, lo = map_dbl(ci, "lo") / 10,
         hi = map_dbl(ci, "hi") / 10,
         outcome = factor(outcome, levels = rev(outcomes))) |>
  ggplot(aes(est, outcome, colour = kit_type)) +
  geom_pointrange(aes(xmin = lo, xmax = hi),
                  position = position_dodge(width = 0.55), fatten = 2.5) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::label_percent(scale = 1),
                     limits = c(0, NA)) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("#3B6E8F", "#C46A1B"), name = NULL) +
  labs(x = paste0("Share of 500 kit recipients (", ci_pct, " Wilson CI)"),
       y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), legend.position = "top")
```


```{r}
#| label: kit-covariate-prep
#| include: false
# Covariate descriptives for the 500 kit recipients. Two nested return outcomes:
# returned = card came back & assessed (154); valid = >=1 valid blood spot (117).
kitc <- read_csv("master_dataset_noaddr.csv", show_col_types = FALSE,
                 name_repair = "minimal") |>
  filter(!is.na(treatment_kit)) |>
  mutate(
    returned = !is.na(DBSLvalidation) | !is.na(DBSRvalidation),
    valid    = valid_sample >= 1,
    disadv = factor(if_else(disadvantaged, "Disadvantaged", "Not disadvantaged"),
                    levels = c("Not disadvantaged", "Disadvantaged")),
    tenure = factor(case_when(
               str_starts(home, "Owned")            ~ "Owned",
               str_detect(home, "private landlord") ~ "Rented (private)",
               str_detect(home, "council")          ~ "Rented (council)"),
             levels = c("Owned", "Rented (private)", "Rented (council)")),
    educ = factor(case_when(
             str_detect(highest_education, "Undergraduate|Postgraduate") ~ "Degree",
             str_detect(highest_education, "age 18|Post-school")         ~ "A-level / vocational",
             str_detect(highest_education, "age 16|No formal")           ~ "<=GCSE / none"),
           levels = c("Degree", "A-level / vocational", "<=GCSE / none")),
    inc = factor(case_when(
            income %in% c("Under £15,000", "£15,000 - £24,999")        ~ "< £25k",
            income %in% c("£25,000 - £34,999", "£35,000 - £49,999")    ~ "£25-50k",
            income %in% c("£50,000 - £74,999", "£75,000 - £99,999")    ~ "£50-100k",
            income %in% c("£100,000 - £149,999", "£150,000 and above") ~ "£100k+"),
          levels = c("£100k+", "£50-100k", "£25-50k", "< £25k")),
    worry = factor(worried_exposure,
             levels = c("Not worried", "A little worried", "Quite worried", "Very worried")),
    knew = factor(prior_lead_knowledge, levels = c("Yes", "No", "Not sure")),
    letter = factor(treatment_parsed, levels = c("C", "G", "P"),
             labels = c("Control", "Generalized", "Personalized")))

# Rate + Wilson CI (in %) for one categorical var, both return outcomes; drop tiny cats.
kit_forest <- function(var, min_n = 10) {
  kitc |>
    filter(!is.na(.data[[var]])) |>
    pivot_longer(c(returned, valid), names_to = "outcome", values_to = "hit") |>
    mutate(outcome = factor(outcome, levels = c("returned", "valid"),
                            labels = c("Returned any kit", ">=1 valid spot"))) |>
    group_by(level = .data[[var]], outcome) |>
    summarise(x = sum(hit), n = n(), .groups = "drop") |>
    filter(n >= min_n) |>
    rowwise() |>
    mutate(ci = list(score_ci(x, n, cl = ci_level))) |>
    ungroup() |>
    mutate(est = map_dbl(ci, "est") / 10, lo = map_dbl(ci, "lo") / 10,
           hi = map_dbl(ci, "hi") / 10)
}

plot_kit_forest <- function(df) {
  ggplot(df, aes(est, fct_rev(level), colour = outcome)) +
    geom_pointrange(aes(xmin = lo, xmax = hi),
                    position = position_dodge(width = 0.55), fatten = 2.2) +
    scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::label_percent(scale = 1), limits = c(0, NA)) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = c("#3B6E8F", "#C46A1B"), name = NULL) +
    labs(x = paste0("Share of kit recipients (", ci_pct, " Wilson CI)"), y = NULL) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), legend.position = "top")
}
```

## Disadvantaged participants less likely to return kits \hfill \small \alert{(Preliminary)}

```{r}
#| label: kit-return-disadvantaged
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 2.4
plot_kit_forest(kit_forest("disadv"))
```

## Disadvantaged participants less likely to return kits \hfill \small \alert{(Preliminary)}

```{r}
#| label: kit-return-disadv-diff
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 2.6
# Score (Newcombe) interval for a difference of proportions, built from the two
# Wilson score intervals. Contrast = disadvantaged - not disadvantaged.
wilson_lohi <- function(x, n) {
  z <- qnorm(1 - (1 - ci_level) / 2); p <- x / n
  ctr <- (p + z^2 / (2 * n)) / (1 + z^2 / n)
  h   <- z * sqrt(p * (1 - p) / n + z^2 / (4 * n^2)) / (1 + z^2 / n)
  c(lo = ctr - h, hi = ctr + h)
}
score_diff <- function(x1, n1, x0, n0) {
  p1 <- x1 / n1; p0 <- x0 / n0; w1 <- wilson_lohi(x1, n1); w0 <- wilson_lohi(x0, n0)
  d <- p1 - p0
  tibble(diff = d,
         lo = unname(d - sqrt((p1 - w1["lo"])^2 + (w0["hi"] - p0)^2)),
         hi = unname(d + sqrt((w1["hi"] - p1)^2 + (p0 - w0["lo"])^2)))
}
outc <- c("Returned any kit" = "returned", ">=1 valid spot" = "valid")
imap_dfr(outc, \(col, lab) {
  d1 <- filter(kitc, disadv == "Disadvantaged")
  d0 <- filter(kitc, disadv == "Not disadvantaged")
  score_diff(sum(d1[[col]]), nrow(d1), sum(d0[[col]]), nrow(d0)) |>
    mutate(outcome = lab)
}) |>
  mutate(outcome = factor(outcome, levels = rev(names(outc))),
         across(c(diff, lo, hi), \(x) 100 * x)) |>
  ggplot(aes(diff, outcome)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey55") +
  geom_pointrange(aes(xmin = lo, xmax = hi), colour = "#3B6E8F",
                  fatten = 3, linewidth = 0.8) +
  labs(x = "Disadvantaged - not disadvantaged (percentage points)", y = NULL,
       caption = paste0(ci_pct, " Newcombe score interval")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        plot.caption = element_text(size = 9, colour = "grey40"))
```


## Housing Tenure and Education Predict Return Rates \hfill \small \alert{(Preliminary)} 

```{r}
#| label: kit-return-ses
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 4.1
bind_rows(
  kit_forest("tenure") |> mutate(panel = "Housing tenure"),
  kit_forest("educ")   |> mutate(panel = "Education")) |>
  mutate(level = factor(level, levels = c("Owned", "Rented (private)", "Rented (council)",
                                          "Degree", "A-level / vocational", "<=GCSE / none")),
         panel = factor(panel, levels = c("Housing tenure", "Education"))) |>
  plot_kit_forest() +
  facet_grid(panel ~ ., scales = "free_y", space = "free_y")
```



## Income and Kit Return Rates \hfill \small \alert{(Preliminary)}

```{r}
#| label: kit-return-income
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 3.0
plot_kit_forest(kit_forest("inc"))
```

## Worry / Prior Lead Knowledge and Return Rates \hfill \small \alert{(Preliminary)}

```{r}
#| label: kit-return-attitudes
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 4.1
bind_rows(
  kit_forest("worry") |> mutate(panel = "Worried about lead?"),
  kit_forest("knew")  |> mutate(panel = "Knew about lead beforehand?")) |>
  mutate(level = factor(level, levels = c("Not worried", "A little worried",
                                          "Quite worried", "Very worried",
                                          "Yes", "No", "Not sure")),
         panel = factor(panel, levels = c("Worried about lead?",
                                          "Knew about lead beforehand?"))) |>
  plot_kit_forest() +
  facet_grid(panel ~ ., scales = "free_y", space = "free_y")
```



<!--## Feedback survey
 
- It was opened a few days ago we think? Ask Andrea
- Perhaps we could present some very preliminary results?-->


## Categorizing letters

- A few hundred outreach letters were returned to Warwick
- We have an RA going through all of them; hasn't finished yet 
- Check to make sure that LSOA characteristics aren't related to returned letters; correct response rate estimates


# Appendix

## Sampling Design: Exclusion / De-duplication

\hypertarget{appx-exclusion}{}

\hfill\hyperlink{main-datasources}{\beamerreturnbutton{Back}}


\vspace{-2em }
### Addresses Excluded 
- Hotels / student residences: 936
- Vacant properties: 931
- Missing UPRN: 73 
- Missing LSOA: 524
- Missing postcode (after EPC fallback): 3491

### De-duplication
- After exclusion, 18 UPRNs appear *exactly twice*  
- These represent errors in the Council Tax data
- Only affects address; keep first one from each pair

### Final Sampling Frame: 370,666 rows
- Address 1-4, postcode, UPRN
- OA (2,607), LSOA (488), MSOA (107)
- EPC Housing age: "Old" if $\leq 1966$ (missing for 38.5\%)
- \# Households in LSOA $\geq$ one child aged $\leq 9$ (2021 Census)

## What is the Index of Multiple Deprivation (IMD)?

\hypertarget{appx-imd}{}

\hfill\hyperlink{main-deprivation}{\beamerreturnbutton{Back}}

- Official England-wide deprivation measure, published by the Ministry of Housing, Communities \& Local Government; scored for each LSOA ($\approx$ 1,500 residents)
- Combines **seven domains**: income; employment; education \& skills; health \& disability; crime; barriers to housing \& services; living environment
- Higher score = more deprived; we use **IMD 2025** (2021 LSOA boundaries, matching our Census)
- Here: rank Leeds LSOAs by deprivation and split into **quintiles** (equal fifths of LSOAs; Q1 = least, Q5 = most deprived)

## Distribution of LSOA deprivation: England vs Leeds

\hypertarget{appx-imd-dist}{}

\hfill\hyperlink{main-deprivation}{\beamerreturnbutton{Back}}

\vspace{-2em}
:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="56%"}

```{r}
#| label: imd-england-vs-leeds
#| fig-width: 4.8
#| fig-height: 4.8
#| out-width: 100%
library(patchwork)

# England (all LSOAs, including Leeds) vs Leeds alone, on a shared score axis.
eng   <- read_csv("../data/england-lsoa-imd-2025-scores.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
xlims <- range(eng$imd_score)

dens <- function(df, ttl, fillcol, xlab = NULL) {
  ggplot(df, aes(imd_score)) +
    geom_density(fill = fillcol, colour = NA) +
    coord_cartesian(xlim = xlims) +
    labs(title = ttl, x = xlab, y = NULL) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(axis.text.y = element_blank(),
          panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
          plot.title = element_text(size = 12))
}

dens(eng, "England — 33,755 LSOAs", "grey65") /
  dens(filter(eng, lsoa %in% lsoa_imd$lsoa), "Leeds — 488 LSOAs", "#B5651D",
       xlab = "IMD score  (higher = more deprived)")
```

:::

::: {.column width="40%"}

\vspace{2em}

\footnotesize

```{r}
#| label: imd-england-vs-leeds-table
#| results: asis
tab <- read_csv("../data/england-lsoa-imd-2025-scores.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  mutate(leeds = lsoa %in% lsoa_imd$lsoa, dec = ntile(-imd_score, 10))

fmt <- function(d) c(
  sprintf("%.1f", median(d$imd_score)),
  sprintf("%.0f%%", 100 * mean(d$dec == 1)),
  sprintf("%.0f%%", 100 * mean(d$dec == 10)),
  sprintf("%.0f%%", 100 * mean(d$dec %in% 4:7)))

tibble(
  Measure = c("Median IMD score",
              "Most-deprived decile",
              "Least-deprived decile",
              "Middle (deciles 4--7)"),
  England = fmt(tab),
  Leeds   = fmt(filter(tab, leeds))
) |>
  knitr::kable(format = "latex", booktabs = TRUE, align = "lrr", linesep = "",
               col.names = c("", "England", "Leeds"))
```

:::

::::

## Quintiles of Deprivation in Leeds: by LSOA

\hypertarget{appx-imd-map}{}

\hfill\hyperlink{main-deprivation}{\beamerreturnbutton{Back}}

\vspace{-2em}

```{r}
#| label: leeds-imd-map
#| fig-width: 6.6
#| fig-height: 4
leeds_lsoa <- leeds_geo |>
  left_join(lsoa_imd, by = c("LSOA21CD" = "lsoa")) |>
  mutate(quintile = factor(bin_imd(imd_score),
                           labels = c("Q1 least", "Q2", "Q3", "Q4", "Q5 most")))

ggplot(leeds_lsoa, aes(fill = quintile)) +
  geom_sf(colour = "white", linewidth = 0.05) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "YlOrBr", direction = 1, na.value = "grey85",
                    name = "IMD quintile") +
  coord_sf(datum = NA) +
  theme_void(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "right")
```

## Heterogeneity: does the gradient differ by treatment? (Old)

\hypertarget{appx-imd-treat}{}

\hfill\hyperlink{main-by-age}{\beamerreturnbutton{Back}}

```{r}
#| label: imd-by-treatment
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 3.5
imd_cells |>
  filter(age_grp == "Old") |>
  imd_summary("treatment") |>
  mutate(treatment = recode(treatment, C = "Control", G = "Generalized",
                            P = "Personalized")) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = q, y = rate, colour = treatment, group = treatment)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.6, position = position_dodge(0.12)) +
  geom_pointrange(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), size = 0.4,
                  position = position_dodge(0.12)) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("Control" = "#666666",
                                 "Generalized" = "#3B6E8F",
                                 "Personalized" = "#B5651D")) +
  labs(x = "IMD quintile  (left = least deprived)",
       y = "Usable per 1,000", colour = NULL,
       caption = paste0("Old houses only; ", ci_caption)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        plot.caption = element_text(size = 9, colour = "grey40"),
        plot.margin = margin(t = 10, r = 8, b = 4, l = 6))
```

\footnotesize \hyperlink{appx-click-treat}{\beamergotobutton{Clicks version of this plot}}


## Empirical Bayes Estimation of LSOA Response Rates 

For LSOA $\ell$ with $n_\ell$ letters and $y_\ell$ usable responses:

### Model
$y_\ell \mid p_\ell \sim \text{Binomial}(n_\ell, p_\ell), \qquad p_\ell \sim \text{Beta}(a, b)$ — the shared prior is what lets sparse areas borrow strength.


### Conjugate Posterior
**Posterior** (conjugate). 
$p_\ell \mid y_\ell \sim \text{Beta}(y_\ell + a,\ n_\ell - y_\ell + b)$, so

$$\hat p_\ell = \frac{y_\ell + a}{n_\ell + a + b} = w_\ell\,\frac{y_\ell}{n_\ell} + (1 - w_\ell)\,\frac{a}{a + b}, \qquad w_\ell = \frac{n_\ell}{n_\ell + a + b}$$


### Estimation
Choose $(a, b)$ to maximise the marginal Beta-Binomial likelihood $\;\prod_\ell \int \text{Bin}(y_\ell \mid n_\ell, p)\,\text{Beta}(p \mid a, b)\,dp\;$, then plug in: here $\hat a \approx$ `r round(eb_a)`, $\hat b \approx$ `r round(eb_b)`.

## Shrinkage of LSOA estimates, and the estimated rate distribution

\vspace{-1em}

```{r}
#| label: eb-shrinkage
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 4.3
#| out-width: 88%
library(patchwork)
xmax <- max(eb_lsoa$raw)

funnel <- ggplot(eb_lsoa) +
  geom_segment(aes(x = raw, xend = post, y = 1, yend = 0),
               colour = "#3B6E8F", alpha = 0.12, linewidth = 0.2) +
  geom_point(aes(raw,  1), colour = "#B5651D", size = 0.5, alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_point(aes(post, 0), colour = "#3B6E8F", size = 0.5, alpha = 0.5) +
  annotate("text", x = 0, y = 1, label = "raw  y/n", hjust = 0, vjust = -1.1, size = 3.3) +
  annotate("text", x = 0, y = 0, label = "shrunk",   hjust = 0, vjust = 2.1,  size = 3.3) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, xmax)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-0.4, 1.4), breaks = NULL) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_blank())

prior <- tibble(rate = seq(0, xmax, length.out = 400)) |>
  mutate(dens = dbeta(rate / 1000, eb_a, eb_b))
distp <- ggplot(prior, aes(rate, dens)) +
  geom_area(fill = "#3B6E8F", alpha = 0.85) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, xmax)) +
  labs(x = "Usable per 1,000", y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), axis.text.y = element_blank())

funnel / distp + plot_layout(heights = c(1.1, 1))
```

\vspace{-1em}

\small Top: each LSOA's raw rate (upper row) joined to its empirical-Bayes estimate (lower row). Bottom: the fitted Beta prior --- the estimated distribution of true rates.

## Usable response rate by LSOA: raw

\hypertarget{appx-eb}{}

\hfill\hyperlink{main-deprivation}{\beamerreturnbutton{Back}}

```{r}
#| label: eb-map-raw
#| fig-width: 6
#| fig-height: 4.2
#| out-width: 62%
leeds_geo |>
  left_join(eb_lsoa, by = c("LSOA21CD" = "lsoa")) |>
  ggplot(aes(fill = raw)) +
  geom_sf(colour = "white", linewidth = 0.02) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "rocket", direction = -1, name = "Usable\nper 1,000") +
  coord_sf(datum = NA) +
  theme_void(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "right")
```

\small $y/n$ per LSOA: 0--23 per 1,000, with 117 of 488 LSOAs at zero --- mostly sampling noise.

## Usable response rate by LSOA: empirical-Bayes shrunk

```{r}
#| label: eb-map-shrunk
#| fig-width: 6
#| fig-height: 4.2
#| out-width: 62%
leeds_geo |>
  left_join(eb_lsoa, by = c("LSOA21CD" = "lsoa")) |>
  ggplot(aes(fill = post)) +
  geom_sf(colour = "white", linewidth = 0.02) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "mako", direction = -1, name = "Usable\nper 1,000") +
  coord_sf(datum = NA) +
  theme_void(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "right")
```

\small Posterior means --- *own scale (4--9) and a different palette*. Far narrower range: most LSOAs pulled to the Leeds average.

## Clicking: deprivation gradient by housing age \hfill \small \alert{(Preliminary)}

\hypertarget{appx-click-age}{}

\hfill\hyperlink{main-by-age}{\beamerreturnbutton{Back}}

```{r}
#| label: click-imd-by-age
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 3.3
imd_summary(imd_cells, "age_grp", outcome = "clicks") |>
  mutate(age_grp = factor(age_grp, levels = c("Old", "New", "Unknown"))) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = q, y = rate, colour = age_grp, group = age_grp)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.6, position = position_dodge(0.12)) +
  geom_pointrange(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), size = 0.45,
                  position = position_dodge(0.12)) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("Old" = "#B5651D", "New" = "#3B6E8F",
                                 "Unknown" = "#7A7A7A")) +
  labs(x = "IMD quintile  (left = least deprived)",
       y = "Clicks per 1,000", colour = NULL, caption = ci_caption) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        plot.caption = element_text(size = 9, colour = "grey40"),
        plot.margin = margin(t = 10, r = 8, b = 4, l = 6))
```

## Clicking: does the gradient differ by treatment? (Old) \hfill \small \alert{(Preliminary)}

\hypertarget{appx-click-treat}{}

\hfill\hyperlink{appx-imd-treat}{\beamerreturnbutton{Back}}

```{r}
#| label: click-imd-by-treatment
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 3.3
imd_cells |>
  filter(age_grp == "Old") |>
  imd_summary("treatment", outcome = "clicks") |>
  mutate(treatment = recode(treatment, C = "Control", G = "Generalized",
                            P = "Personalized")) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = q, y = rate, colour = treatment, group = treatment)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.6, position = position_dodge(0.12)) +
  geom_pointrange(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), size = 0.4,
                  position = position_dodge(0.12)) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("Control" = "#666666",
                                 "Generalized" = "#3B6E8F",
                                 "Personalized" = "#B5651D")) +
  labs(x = "IMD quintile  (left = least deprived)",
       y = "Clicks per 1,000", colour = NULL,
       caption = paste0("Old houses only; ", ci_caption)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        plot.caption = element_text(size = 9, colour = "grey40"),
        plot.margin = margin(t = 10, r = 8, b = 4, l = 6))
```
